findings: Graph rebuilt with citation_builder.py reconnection (344 paper_cites_paper edges), reprocessing_candidate_builder.py (1 dataset flagged), and expanded file validation (top-300, 162 file_validated upgrades, up from top-50).
notes: Graph grew from 12748/149654 to 12748/149998 (+344 edges). No regression.

findings: citation_builder.py: RECONNECTED this session (was orphan, node-ID-scheme bug fixed, now merged via orphaned_layers, 344 real paper_cites_paper edges).
reprocessing_candidate_builder.py: NEW this session, merged via attach_reprocessing_candidate_status build-time step, 1 dataset flagged.
ner_builder.py: confirmed still side-channel (artifacts/ner/ner_kg.jsonl read by search_features._load_ner_index()).
neurosynth_builder.py: confirmed still side-channel with fallback (per prior audit).
atlas_builder.py: reachable via apps/api/atlas_router.py (a dedicated API route, not the KG merge path) -- not orphaned.
openalex_citations.py: standalone CLI script (has __main__), likely the upstream producer of artifacts/citations/citation_edges.jsonl -- not orphaned, operational-script category.

paper_linking.py: reachable via neural_search/core/query.py -> retrieval.py/evaluation/baseline_ladder.py -- UNCLEAR whether neural_search/core/* is on the production apps/api/main.py search path or is a separate evaluation-only retrieval implementation. Flagged as needing follow-up, not resolved.

semantic_edges.py: NEW FINDING -- zero real callers found for add_semantic_edges_to_graph/build_semantic_dataset_edges/load_and_add_semantic_edges/get_semantic_neighbors/build_concept_similarity_edges beyond the module itself and graph/__init__.py re-export. Same dead-code shape as similarity.py (confirmed dead, deleted 2026-07-02). Proposed action: verify with a repo-wide grep one more time, then delete if confirmed, per the similarity.py precedent.
notes: First formal run of this playbook. 2/9 audited modules were genuinely new findings (citation_builder fixed this session; semantic_edges.py newly suspected dead). paper_linking.py reachability needs a follow-up session to resolve definitively.

findings: First formal run of this playbook. Synthesized reports/reanalysis_insight_report.md from the live production graph (12748 nodes / 149998 edges, confirmed non-regressed by benchmark-gatekeeper this cycle).

Top-15 evidence-backed reuse list (dataset_reanalysis_bridge_dataset) required diversity capping (MAX_ROWS_PER_PRECEDENT=2) to avoid degenerating into 15 near-identical rows from one highly-connected NeuroMorpho precedent at a single confidence value (0.36) -- naive top-N by confidence is not a good ranking for this edge type.

Top high-confidence unexplored-candidate list (dataset_old_dataset_new_method_candidate, confidence>=0.85, zero linked papers) similarly required per-technique capping (MAX_ROWS_PER_TECHNIQUE=3); only 9/15 slots filled after capping -- honest signal-breadth finding, not a bug.

Re-tested the reinterpretation-candidate assumption against current data: multi-source paper-linkage growth (403->2510 real matches, 5 sources) resolves only 8 additional OpenAlex-joinable matches by DOI and adds zero new dataset_reanalysis_bridge_dataset edges (2517 either way). dataset_reinterpretation_candidate remains 0. Root cause identified precisely: ner_kg.jsonl method-mention extraction has only ever run against OpenAlex-ingested paper text, not Crossref/PubMed/DataCite records -- this is the real bottleneck, not paper-linkage breadth.

Added neural_search.graph.reanalysis_bridge_builder.load_dataset_paper_matches_multi_source() as a small, tested, reusable utility for this check (9 new tests total across test_reanalysis_bridge_builder.py, test_context_brief.py, test_generate_reanalysis_insight_report.py) -- kept despite the null result since the finding itself is now precisely on record.
notes: Also shipped this session: neural_search/agents/context_brief.py (single current-state briefing every playbook should read first) and the two previously-missing playbooks (file_validation_runner.md, literature_linker.md), so all 5 registered agents now have a runnable playbook.
